2605.11518.title: AutoLLMResearch: Training Research Agents for Automating LLM Experiment Configuration -- Learning from Cheap, Optimizing Expensive
2605.11518.paper_summary: Effectively configuring scalable large language model (LLM) experiments, spanning architecture design, hyperparameter tuning, and beyond, is crucial for advancing LLM research, as poor configuration choices can waste substantial computational resources and prevent models from realizing their full potential. Prior automated methods are designed for low-cost settings where repeated trial and error is feasible, but scalable LLM experiments are too expensive for such extensive iteration. To our knowledge, no work has addressed the automation of high-cost LLM experiment configurations, leaving this problem labor-intensive and dependent on expert intuition. Motivated by this gap, we propose AutoLLMResearch, an agentic framework that mimics how human researchers learn generalizable principles from low-fidelity experiments and extrapolate to efficiently identify promising configurations in expensive LLM settings. The core challenge is how to enable an agent to learn, through interaction with a multi-fidelity experimental environment that captures the structure of the LLM configuration landscape. To achieve this, we propose a systematic framework with two key components: 1) LLMConfig-Gym, a multi-fidelity environment encompassing four critical LLM experiment tasks, supported by over one million GPU hours of verifiable experiment outcomes; 2) A structured training pipeline that formulates configuration research as a long-horizon Markov Decision Process and accordingly incentivizes cross-fidelity extrapolation reasoning. Extensive evaluation against diverse strong baselines on held-out experiments demonstrates the effectiveness, generalization, and interpretability of our framework, supporting its potential as a practical and general solution for scalable real-world LLM experiment automation.
2605.11518.paper_summary_zh: 有效配置可擴展的大型語言模型（LLM）實驗，包括架構設計、超參數調整等，對於推進LLM研究至關重要，因為不當的配置選擇可能浪費大量計算資源，並阻礙模型發揮其全部潛力。先前的自動化方法是為低成本環境設計的，在這種環境中，重複的試錯是可行的，但可擴展的LLM實驗對於如此廣泛的迭代來說成本過高。據我們所知，尚未有工作解決高成本LLM實驗配置的自動化問題，這使得該問題仍然需要大量人力並依賴專家的直覺。受到這一空白的啟發，我們提出了AutoLLMResearch，一個模仿人類研究者如何從低保真實驗中學習可概括原則並推斷以有效識別高成本LLM環境中有前景配置的代理框架。核心挑戰在於如何使代理通過與捕捉LLM配置景觀結構的多保真實驗環境互動來學習。為了實現這一目標，我們提出了一個系統化框架，包含兩個關鍵組件：1）LLMConfig-Gym，一個包含四個關鍵LLM實驗任務的多保真環境，支持超過一百萬小時可驗證的實驗結果；2）一個結構化的訓練管道，將配置研究表述為長期馬可夫決策過程，並相應地激勵跨保真推斷推理。對保留實驗進行的廣泛評估顯示了我們框架的有效性、泛化能力和可解釋性，支持其作為可擴展現實世界LLM實驗自動化的實用和通用解決方案的潛力。
2605.11518.author: Taicheng Guo et.al.
2605.11518.authors: Taicheng Guo, Nitesh V. Chawla, Olaf Wiest, Xiangliang Zhang
